abstract.text: This article describes the KeyboardKit callout engine.

inlineContent.text: Callouts are an important part of the typing experience, where an
can show the currently pressed key and an
can show secondary actions when certain keys are long pressed.
👑 KeyboardKit Pro unlocks localized secondary callout actions for all
locales that are included in the license. You can sign up for KeyboardKit Pro from the

namespace that contains callout-related types and views, like callout

and
views, callout-related styles, etc.

or the
that should be presented in callouts above the keyboard.

KeyboardKit automatically creates an instance of this class and registers it with its
, and also injects it into the view environment.
An
can be presented from a pressed key to zoom in on its character. This is by default only used on iPhone devices, while iPad devices by default changes the color of the pressed key.
An
can be presented from a long pressed key, to show secondary callout
for the currently pressed key.
KeyboardKit will by default use the
function to resolve the standard callout actions for all
locales. You can use the
modifier to customize callout actions for any action:

code: // See the demo app for a better, interactive example.
... // Insert the view to use here, for instance a KeyboardView

inlineContent.text: You can customize callouts for any actions, then use the
to return the standard actions for all other actions.
You can use the
view modifier to customize the style of the input and action callouts and their items.
The
namespace has callout-specific views, that can be used to present input and action callouts that mimic native callouts.
The
view can present secondary actions when a key with secondary actions is long-pressed.
The user can swipe left/right while pressing to move the selection.
The
view can be used to zoom in the currently pressed input key as the user types.
KeyboardKit Pro unlocks localized callout
for all
locales in your license. It will automatically add them to the
You can access these localized callout actions with the optional values like
, and
, or by using the throwing builders, like
. These builders will fail if the locale is not included in your license.
